deferred_items.rationale: User-confirmed (option A). Underlying gap: 0% test coverage on 839-line reference implementation. Immediate workaround: added to .test-baseline.json to unblock CI. Proper fix (comprehensive node:test suite mocking state files + exercising all 9 dimensions + security helpers + JSON/human modes) belongs in its own PR — DEBT-45655 tracks. Single canonical disposition: deferred (the baseline addition is just the workaround, not the fix).

rejected_items.item: Qodo Compliance i1 — PATH-based execution (now also covers git)
rejected_items.rationale: Cross-round dedup: extends R1 Qodo #2 rejection rationale to git grep. Same justification: hardcoded args (no untrusted input concatenation), trusted PATH (developer/CI environment), no exploit window. Documented inline in R1 commit; same rationale applies.

rejected_items.item: Qodo Compliance i2 — Path disclosure in error messages
rejected_items.rationale: User-accepted (option A). statePath/skillDir in error messages are useful for debugging in dev/CI context. Script is run by developers with access to the project anyway; informational ⚪ severity confirms this is a soft observation, not a vulnerability.

rejected_items.item: Qodo Compliance i3 — Unstructured console logs
rejected_items.rationale: User-accepted (option A). printHumanStream is intentionally human-readable; --json flag (already exists at L101-104) covers structured-output use case for centralized log ingestion. Separation of concerns: one mode per output target.
